<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="outer" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#f4f0ff"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#d8ccff"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="inner" x1="0" y1="1" x2="1" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#ffe7d6"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#ffd1b0"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Mitochondrion outer membrane (bean/elliptical) -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="260" rx="180" ry="120" fill="url(#outer)" stroke="#7a5cff" stroke-width="6"/>
  <!-- Outer ring hint -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="260" rx="155" ry="98" fill="none" stroke="#5a38ff" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- Inner matrix -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="260" rx="140" ry="88" fill="url(#inner)" stroke="#ff8a4a" stroke-width="3"/>

  <!-- Inner membrane cristae (folds) -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#7a2fff" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.95">
    <path d="M185 210 C220 185, 250 182, 286 190"/>
    <path d="M170 240 C215 214, 260 208, 304 220"/>
    <path d="M168 270 C220 248, 270 246, 312 260"/>
    <path d="M176 300 C220 288, 270 292, 306 306"/>
    <path d="M192 325 C225 322, 255 330, 285 340"/>
  </g>

  <g fill="none" stroke="#b64dff" stroke-width="3.5" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M210 205 C235 194, 265 196, 286 205"/>
    <path d="M198 235 C238 220, 275 220, 300 232"/>
    <path d="M198 270 C235 260, 275 262, 300 272"/>
    <path d="M206 305 C240 300, 273 306, 292 316"/>
    <path d="M220 335 C245 336, 265 344, 280 352"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Slight highlight edges for depth -->
  <path d="M168 250 C190 170, 300 150, 342 230" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="8" opacity="0.35"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating a guessing game image**

I need to design a one-word guessing game involving mitochondria, specifically drawing a mitochondrion-like shape. It should be bean-shaped, featuring an inner membrane with cristae and no letters. I’ll create this using ellipses and paths in an SVG format of 512x512. I'll include an outer double membrane, represented by two ellipses, and curved lines for the inner folds. Using a gradient could be nice, but I'll keep it simple with strokes and fills, ensuring there’s no text. Finally, I'll output this as JSON.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

**Identifying drawing elements**

I'm thinking the drawing resembles an eye, perhaps focusing on the iris or pupil. It's shaped like an oval with lines that look like eyelashes, so I'd lean toward "eye" as the most confident guess. I should consider alternatives like eyeball, iris, pupil, retina, and contact lens. However, I need to come up with one-word guesses without repeating myself. I should aim to provide around 20 options for clarity and variety!
